risks.blocking: Blocks new cross-module private dependencies from being accepted after S01.
risks.summary: Backend has 204 cross-module imports, including 47 foreign infrastructure imports, without an automatic boundary gate.

risks.blocking: Blocks PROJECT-PLATFORM-S05 because layout, publish, and WorkItem runtime would expand the same private dependencies.
risks.summary: Project directly imports identity, file, platform, event, audit, permission, and IM private layers.

risks.blocking: Shared code cannot remain a stable technical dependency while it imports business providers.
risks.summary: Three shared imports point to identity infrastructure or knowledge application.

risks.blocking: New foreign writes or ownerless tables must fail before S01 closes.
risks.summary: The current schema has 85 active tables and Java contains 96 cross-owner file-table candidates in 22 files.

risks.blocking: Contract extraction must preserve atomic outbox/audit behavior and expose other cross-owner writes explicitly.
risks.summary: Sixteen transactional application files import foreign infrastructure.
One Spring server hosts HTTP API, worker polling, general WebSocket sessions, and legacy collaboration schedules.

risks.blocking: Blocks independent worker scaling and crash recovery.
risks.summary: DomainEventWorker is scheduled in the API process and has no lease/dead-letter/replay runtime boundary.
General WebSocket and legacy Spring knowledge collaboration retain six process-memory state locations.

risks.blocking: Does not block S01; blocks publishing an actual capacity commitment.
risks.summary: No fixed multi-role load, soak, failure, or recovery baseline exists.
